goal: Fix two pre-existing PBT flakes: chronological-replay date sort + auto-pin-on-sync test obsolescence

summary.title: Fix two pre-existing PBT flakes (date sort + obsolete test API)
summary.what: Two test failures from `make test` on main, called out as follow-ups in PR #29. (A) TestPropertyRevertNoteShape: scanMainNotes used sort.Slice (unstable) on second-precision commit dates, so two same-second commits could apply in either order, breaking the chronological-replay invariant. Switched to sort.SliceStable + topological-order tiebreaker via a new gitops.RevListOrder helper (RevListSet now wraps it). (B) TestPropertyReconcileIdempotent: pre-v0.2 test that called svc.Pin() after svc.Sync(), but v0.2 made Sync auto-pin. By the time Pin runs, theres nothing left. Replaced the three Pin() assertions with three Sync() assertions checking SyncResult.AutoPinned — same idempotency invariant, asserted at the surface users actually observe.
summary.why: Both flakes were tracked as follow-ups when PR #29 split them out to keep the perf PR focused. The mainline-watch user is about to do MVP rollout and `make test` failing on a fresh checkout would block them immediately. Fixing now, before handoff, costs 90 LOC across 3 files and removes a real correctness flake (revert-after-merge same-second case) plus a stale test that no longer matches v0.2 semantics.
summary.user_goal: Make `make test` green on a fresh checkout so the friend can ship MVP without chasing pre-existing flakes.

decisions.point: Topological-order tiebreaker vs commit hash tiebreaker
decisions.rationale: Hash tiebreaker is deterministic but semantically arbitrary; same-second commits would be sorted by SHA prefix. Topological order matches user intent: when two commits land in the same wall-clock second, the parent applied first by definition. Tying to git history reflects that — the further-from-HEAD commit is older.

decisions.point: New RevListOrder helper vs inline rev-list call in sync
decisions.chose: helper in gitops, used by RevListSet wrapper
decisions.rationale: Keep gitops the single owner of the rev-list shape, callers consume either a set or an ordered slice as needed. RevListSet still works (callers that only need reachability) — its now a thin wrapper. Zero compatibility break.

decisions.point: TestPropertyReconcileIdempotent: assert AutoPinned vs add explicit Pin escape
decisions.rationale: AutoPin IS the user-observable surface in v0.2. Adding an escape hatch to call Pin without auto-pin would create a test-only path that no real user reaches; would also test something different from production behaviour.

rejected.alternative: Bump commit dates to nanosecond precision
rejected.reason: Would require changing how seal events record timestamps and the meaning of git author dates — cross-cutting and not justified for a single test flake.

rejected.alternative: Add a test-only Pin function bypassing AutoPinAfterSync
rejected.reason: Production-divergent test paths are a code smell; the test should observe what users observe.

rejected.alternative: Keep RevListSet as standalone, add separate RevListOrder
rejected.reason: Same data, computed twice if both ever called. Single source-of-truth is cheaper and prevents drift.

risks: Topological tiebreaker uses RevListOrder over the entire main history. For a 50k-commit main thats ~150ms (already noted in v0.3 spec); same as before just keeping the slice. No regression.

sort.SliceStable preserves input order for equal keys; if the order from `git notes list` ever changes (different git version, --no-pager difference), test outcomes for non-main pairs could shift. The note bodies themselves are deterministic content, so the apply outcome should still be correct — just any non-determinism the test expected from sort.Slice would now be the stable order. Risk is minimal.

Renamed test method semantics (Pin -> Sync) means the test name still says "Reconcile" but tests the AutoPinned surface. Considered renaming the test, decided not to — the underlying semantic invariant (idempotent catch-up) is still what is asserted, and grep-history matters more than perfect naming.
followups: Consider renaming TestPropertyReconcileIdempotent to TestPropertyAutoPinIdempotent in a doc-only follow-up — clarifies what is being tested at the surface name.
If make test still surfaces flakes after this lands, check whether the seedMergedIntent helper is also subject to second-precision races in other PBT tests.

architectural_claims: scanMainNotes chronological-replay sort is now deterministic for same-second commits via topological-order tiebreaker on main.
gitops.RevListSet is a thin wrapper over RevListOrder which preserves git rev-list output order.
PBT test contracts read from SyncResult.AutoPinned — the user-observable v0.2 surface — rather than calling the now-internal Pin path explicitly.
behavioral_changes: TestPropertyRevertNoteShape and TestPropertyReconcileIdempotent now pass deterministically. No production behaviour changes other than the same-second tiebreak being deterministic instead of unstable.
